title: Sitaram Bagh temple
tagline: Sitaram Bagh temple — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: Sitaram Bagh Temple or Seetharambagh temple, is an old temple located in Mangalhat, a suburb of Hyderabad, Telangana in India.
overview.description: Sitaram Bagh Temple or Seetharambagh temple, is an old temple located in Mangalhat, a suburb of Hyderabad, Telangana in India. The temple was built by Seth Puranmal Ganeriwala, who worked as a banker for the Nizams of Hyderabad and was a member of the Ganeriwala family. The temple was built at Mallepally which was then a village at the outskirts of the city. The temple was built with six wells in its premises out of which one is now defunct.

topPlaces.name: Mallepally
topPlaces.description: Mallepally is located near Asifnagar in Hyderabad, India. It is a locality in Hyderabad District and one of the old suburbs that have existed for a long while now.

topPlaces.name: Asif Nagar
topPlaces.description: Asif Nagar is a major neighbourhood in the western part of Hyderabad, India. It is located north of the Musi River near Mehdipatnam.

topPlaces.name: Aghapura
topPlaces.description: Aghapura is a neighbourhood in Hyderabad, India. Located between the major suburbs of Hyderabad, Nampally and Mehdipatnam.

topPlaces.name: Goshamahal
topPlaces.description: Goshamahal is a suburb in Hyderabad City, Telangana, India. Derived from the word gosha meaning secluded women in purdah.

topPlaces.name: Bazarghat
topPlaces.description: Bazarghat is one of the neighbourhoods in Khairatabad, Hyderabad, India. It is connected to the old city of Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Gyan Bagh Palace
topPlaces.description: Gyan Bagh Palace constructed in 1890 is a noble palace constructed in European architecture style, and is located in Hyderabad, India.

topPlaces.name: Dhoolpet
topPlaces.description: Dhoolpet is a neighbourhood in the Old City of Hyderabad India, partly inhabited by people who migrated from Uttar Pradesh during the Nizam rule. Dhoolpet is part of Goshamahal Assembly constituency in old city.

topPlaces.name: A. C. Guards
topPlaces.description: A. C. Guards is a locality in the city of Hyderabad, Telangana. The name A.C Guards came from the African Cavalry Guards of Hyderabad State Forces who were stationed there since the time of sixth Nizam, Mahbub Ali Khan, Asaf Jah VI. The barracks are believed be to have been constructed more than 100 years ago and the quarters of A.C Guards were allocated to the African (Siddi) soldiers of the Nizam's army.

Dhoolpet is a neighbourhood in the Old City of Hyderabad India, partly inhabited by people who migrated from Uttar Pradesh during the Nizam rule. Dhoolpet is part of Goshamahal Assembly constituency in old city.

A. C. Guards is a locality in the city of Hyderabad, Telangana. The name A.C Guards came from the African Cavalry Guards of Hyderabad State Forces who were stationed there since the time of sixth Nizam, Mahbub Ali Khan, Asaf Jah VI. The barracks are believed be to have been constructed more than 100 years ago and the quarters of A.C Guards were allocated to the African (Siddi) soldiers of the Nizam's army.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sitaram Bagh temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sitaram Bagh temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sitaram Bagh temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sitaram Bagh temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sitaram Bagh temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sitaram Bagh temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sitaram Bagh temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sitaram Bagh temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mallepally, Asif Nagar, Aghapura, Goshamahal, Bazarghat.
